system.description: Creates a wall that is 10 feet in length and height, and 1 foot in width. The wall appears in the orientation of the user's choice. It has 20 HP and Armor: 2. The wall crumbles to dust after 1 minute or if it is destroyed.
For 1 Hour, the consumer is Immune to the Restrained and Paralyzed Statuses.
This crimson oil is a bane to scaly-kind. Acts as basic oil. However, while coated on a weapon that is then ignited, its damage against scaled Beings can explode and ignores 2 Armor.

A torch that burns with an unnaturally crimson flame that unsettles Beasts. While burning, Beasts in the Light it sheds are Frightened until they take damage. Afterwards, it can’t be Frightened this way for 1 Day.
This thin blue liquid tightens the consumer’s muscles. For 1 Hour, the consumer’s Speed is increased by 10 feet.

Sheds light out to Close for up to 1 hour while burning and fills the air within Near with a cozy odor. Once per Shift, a Being that takes a Breather in this area gains Favor on its next Will Save that Shift.

A sickly emerald oil that shrieks when lit. Acts as basic oil. However, when coated on a Weapon that is then ignited, attack damage with it can explode if the Target can Cast. If the Target is Focusing and any of the dice do explode, its Focus ends.
For 1 Hour, the consumer can Move vertically up and down in the air as if by Fly, but must gain momentum to Move horizontally.

For 1 Minute, the consumer gains a bonus to Armor and Saves equal to the potion's Rank (+1). Afterward, any Invulnerability Potion the consumer drinks in the next week instead confers this bonus as a penalty.
A flammable, tar-like fluid that ignites when exposed to oxygen, dealing 2d6 damage and Burning (Cd6) a hit Target.
Used in sacred rituals, and is known to be caustic to unholy Beings. Undead and Hellspawn hit with it take d6 damage and are Burning (Cd4).
The consumer of the item regains HP equal to the roll of a countdown die (Cd6). The size of the die is determined by its Rank.
Fragrant bar of fat and oil, used for bathing.
The consumer regains HP equal to a number of d6 rolls determined by the Potion's Rank, plus a modifier equal to its Rank (3d6+3).

When combined with another alchemical item used to make attacks, the catalyst increases the item’s volatility. If the item only Targets a single Being, you can instead Target a point within the same range. If you Pass, all Beings Close to the point are subjected to its effects as if they had been hit with it.

The Target is Sickened (Cd8, it can't knowingly tell a lie).

Consumed: Affects Targets who consume it.
While burning, it sheds Moonlight and counts as a source of ice rather than fire.
The consumer regains HP equal to a number of d6 rolls determined by the Potion's Rank, plus a modifier equal to its Rank (2d6+2).
Removes the Sickened Status from the consumer.
Acts as a torch, but its Light only sheds out to Close.

For 1 Hour, the consumer gains these benefits:

  • They aren't harmed by Blistering Cold.
  • They gain a +2 bonus to Saves against Cold-based attacks and magic.
  • They reduce any Cold damage they take by 1 per die used for the damage.

For 1 Hour, the consumer's body becomes a cloud of gas and the following rules apply:

  • They immediately drop anything they have equipped, and can't equip anything.
  • They can Move by seeping into tiny spaces.
  • They can't Attack or Cast.
  • They are Immune to all damage from attacks that aren't made by Relics.

For 1 Hour, the consumer gains these benefits:

  • They gain a +2 bonus to Saves against being Dazed, Paralyzed, and Shock-based attacks and magic.
  • They reduce any Shock damage they take by 1 per die used for the damage.

The paste can coat either a 5-foot-square area or a Medium or smaller Being for as long as 1 week. When light is shed Near the paste, the paste rapidly crystallizes and emits a shriek audible within 300' for 1 minute. If the light moves out of range, the shriek ends at the end of the Turn. Afterward, the paste is inert.
Used in religious ceremonies, it acts as basic oil. However, when coated on a Weapon that is then ignited, the weapon counts as Silvered.
The stone is known to create an ear-shattering bang, and can be thrown at a Being or a point. Anything Near the Target can't hear until the end of its next Turn.
The consumer of the item regains HP equal to the roll of a countdown die (Cd8). The size of the die is determined by its Rank.
This gold-tipped, iron rod glows brightly when struck. When you do, it sheds Light out to Near for a Shift, and the Light it sheds within Close is Sunlight.

The consumer gains the ability to control a type of Being they can see for 1 Hour, which requires their Focus. Once a Being is chosen, the consumer must make a Will Save. On a pass, the Being is Charmed and under their control for the duration. A Being controlled this way will not obey commands that clearly cause harm to itself. After this time, the Being becomes Hostile. This potion affects Persons (Dwarves, Elves, Humans, Goblins).
A liquid that rapidly freezes when exposed to oxygen. A Target who is hit with this Item takes d6 damage and its Speed is halved until the end of its next Turn.

This thick, brown-tinted smoke is a boon to anyone who needs to go without air for more than a few Rounds. For 1 hour after inhaling the contents of the vial, the Being can hold its breath indefinitely while it’s not Incapacitated.
One dose can cover a Medium or smaller Being for a Shift. During that Shift, that Being is protected against Bitter Cold.
This pungent fluid can be administered by wafting the contents under a Being’s nose. If the Being is Unconscious from sleep, it immediately wakes up.

A special mixture of clay, ash, mandrake root, and the alchemist's own blood. This soup creates a homunculus (p. 120) under the alchemist's control, and it uses their Craft Skill for any Checks it makes. If another homunculus is created from the same alchemist's blood, the oldest homunculus dies.
Sheds light out to Close for up to 1 hour while burning. Insects act as Frightened while within the light it sheds.
This small, wooden stick ignites when struck against a rough surface. Tindertwigs do not burn out over time, meaning it can be reused as often and for as long as the user desires.
A bag filled with sticky black tar that bursts on impact. A Target who is hit with this Item is Restrained (Cd4 Rounds). This has no effect underwater.
Clear oil with a tint of gray wisps seem to swirl through it. Acts as basic oil. However, while coated on a weapon that is then ignited, its attacks can hit incorporeal Beings (like ghosts).

This thick white liquid evaporates almost instantly upon contact with air, creating a thin, nearly invisible smoke. The smoke is nearly odorless and has no effect on most Beings. Undead, however, are repulsed by it. Once used, the smoke fills the area out to Near for 1 minute, or until disbursed by strong wind. Undead are Dazed while in the smoke, ignoring Immunities.

The consumer is transformed as per the Polymorph Spell (p. 97) for 1 Hour. If the consumer isn't aware they are drinking the potion or does not choose a form, roll d66:

d66Beastd66Beastd66Beast
1 1Toad3 1Camel5 1Dolphin
2Sheep2Horse2Pidgeon
3Chicken3Llama3Tiger
4Fox4Cat4Lion
5Boar5Crocodile5Bull
6Hawk6Cow6Shark
2 1Goat4 1Moose6 1Eagle
2Rat2Elk2Bear
3Badger3Giant Spider3Elephant
4Octopus4Viper4Crab
5Rabbit5Wolf5El. Eel
6Owl6Ape6T-rex

For 1 Minute, the consumer gains a bonus to Armor and Saves equal to the potion's Rank (+2). Afterward, any Invulnerability Potion the consumer drinks in the next week instead confers this bonus as a penalty.

Rapidly rusting acid. A hit Target takes d6 damage, plus Burning (Cd6) if made of metal.

If the Target is a non-Relic, the acid destroys a 1-foot circle of it down 1-inch deep, causing the following if it is Armor or a Weapon:

  • Armor is reduced by 1, breaking at 0.
  • Weapons reduce their damage die by one size until repaired. If the damage die is a d4 when this happens, it is destroyed.
For 1 Hour, the consumer becomes Small. During this time, their damage dice are reduced by one size (minimum d4).
While burning, effects that cause Invisibility are suspended while in its Light.
Covers a Medium or Smaller Being for a Shift, during which, insects Near it act as Frightened.

The oil can coat either a 5-foot-square area or a Medium or smaller Target for as long as 1 week unless washed off. While coated, the Target can then be ignited, causing it to be Burning (Cd6, unless doused). Other Beings that touch the flames take d6 damage, and are Burning (d6) while still in contact with the flame. A full flask fuels a burning lantern for a Shift.
For 1 Hour, the consumer can see what another Being of their choice within Far can see. This sense is obstructed by 1 inch of lead or more than 2’ of stone.
The consumer becomes Berserk for a number of Rounds equal to the roll of a countdown die (Cd4). The size of the die is determined by its Rank.

An unstable mass of electric energy that sparks when exposed to oxygen. A Target hit with this Item takes d6 damage and any other Close Beings take half. A Being that takes this damage is Dazed until the end of its next Turn.

A treated wooden stick that can produce a thick plume of opaque smoke. For Cd6, it creates a 10-foot wide column of thick smoke, visible out to one mile outdoors. If thrown indoors, the smoke instead fills up to a 60-foot sphere.

For 1 Hour, the consumer gains these benefits:

  • Their attacks deal an extra die of damage.
  • They treat boulders as Melee Weapons with the Thrown Property (3d6 damage).

A Being can benefit from no more than one of these potions at a time.
Acts as basic oil. However, when coated on a Weapon that is then ignited, it sheds Light out to Near and attacks with it cause the Target to be Burning (Cd4).
For 1 Hour, the consumer gains Telepathy (Far). Using this sense requires Focus, and it is obstructed by 1 inch of lead or more than 2' of stone.
When poured, the viscous gray liquid in this flask instantly forms into a 50-foot-long rope for 1 minute before crumbling to dust.
The consumer becomes Berserk for a number of Rounds equal to the roll of a countdown die (Cd8). The size of the die is determined by its Rank.
The consumer regains HP equal to a number of d6 rolls determined by the Potion's Rank, plus a modifier equal to its Rank (d6+1).
Treated stones with a purple sheen. A hit Target with Darksight is Blinded (Cd6).
For 1 Hour, the consumer and everything they are wearing and carrying become Invisible until they attempt to harm another Being.
When poured on or within an Alchemical Item, the item is reduced back to its raw materials after 1 minute.
This thin blue liquid tightens the consumer’s muscles. For 1 Hour, the consumer’s Speed is increased by 5 feet.

A thick blue oil that acts as a powerful anticoagulant. Acts as basic oil. However, while coated on a weapon that is then ignited, its damage from attacks that hit Beings with half their HP or less can explode and cause them to bleed profusely for the Shift, granting Favor on Survival Checks to track it for this time.

For 1 Minute, the consumer gains a bonus to Armor and Saves equal to the potion's Rank (+3). Afterward, any Invulnerability Potion the consumer drinks in the next week instead confers this bonus as a penalty.
A sticky substance used to adhere materials together. The adhesive can be used on an unsecured Object weighing 10 pounds or less to bond it to anything weighing 10 pounds or more, securing it.
Acts as a torch, but its Light only sheds out to Close. The air in the Light is filled with a fresh, crisp odor. Those in its Light gain Favor on Saves against Berserk or Frightened.

The consumer regains Mana equal to a number of d6 rolls determined by the Potion's Rank, plus a modifier equal to its Rank (2d6+2). Once it regains Mana with one of these potions, it must regain Mana from a Rest to do so again.
The consumer of the item regains HP equal to the roll of a countdown die (Cd4). The size of the die is determined by its Rank.
Can’t be seen with normal vision or beyond Close. When viewed with Darksight, the powder glows. A vial contains enough powder to write a short sentence (10 words or less).

The consumer gains the ability to control a type of Being they can see for 1 Hour, which requires their Focus. Once a Being is chosen, the consumer must make a Will Save. On a pass, the Being is Charmed and under their control for the duration. A Being controlled this way will not obey commands that clearly cause harm to itself. After this time, the Being becomes Hostile. This potion affects Monsters (Dragon, Plant, Giant, Undead).
For 1 Hour, the consumer has a 120' Fly Speed.

The consumer gains the ability to control a type of Being they can see for 1 Hour, which requires their Focus. Once a Being is chosen, the consumer must make a Will Save. On a pass, the Being is Charmed and under their control for the duration. A Being controlled this way will not obey commands that clearly cause harm to itself. After this time, the Being becomes Hostile. This potion affects Beasts.

Contains a green slime (p. 173).

@UUID[Compendium.vagabond.bestiary.Actor.9rpGK7YO9OfbXJoh]{Green Slime}

For 1 Hour, the consumer gains these benefits:

  • Their size increases by one.
  • Their attacks deal an extra die of damage.

A Being can benefit from no more than one of these potions at a time.

The Target is Sickened (Cd4 damage).

Consumed: Affects Targets who consume it.

Injury: Affects Targets who are injured with weapons coated with it.

For 1 Hour, the consumer gains these benefits:

  • They aren't harmed by Sweltering Heat.
  • They gain a +2 bonus to Saves against Fire based attacks and magic.
  • They reduce any Fire damage they take by 1 per die used for the damage.
Corrosive liquid that eats away at matter. A Target hit with it takes d6 damage and is Burning (Cd4).
Gray, smokey vapors that temporarily dull one’s sense of smell when inhaled. A Being that inhales the clearbreath gains Favor on Saves against foul smells and gases for 1 hour.
For 1 Hour, the consumer can hear what other Beings of their choice within Far hear. This sense is obstructed by 1 inch of lead or more than 2’ of stone.
This thin blue liquid tightens the consumer’s muscles. For 1 Hour, the consumer’s Speed is increased by 15 feet.

Vile blighting acid. A hit Target takes d6 damage, plus Burning (Cd6) if made of wood or plants. If the Target is a point, all natural plants Close to it immediately die.

If the Target is a non-Relic, the acid destroys a 1-foot circle of it down 1-inch deep, causing the following if it is Armor or a Weapon:

  • Armor is reduced by 1, breaking at 0.
  • Weapons reduce their damage die by one size until repaired. If the damage die is a d4 when this happens, it is destroyed.

The consumer regains Mana equal to a number of d6 rolls determined by the Potion's Rank, plus a modifier equal to its Rank (d6+1). Once it regains Mana with one of these potions, it must regain Mana from a Rest to do so again.
A fragrant, pleasant spray.

The Target is Sickened (Cd4 damage) and, if it is cursed with Lycanthropy, the curse is removed. In addition to the Material cost, this item requires 1g worth of wolfsbane to Craft.

Consumed: Affects Targets who consume it.

Injury: Affects Targets who are injured with weapons coated with it.
The consumer becomes Berserk for a number of Rounds equal to the roll of a countdown die (Cd6). The size of the die is determined by its Rank.

The consumer regains Mana equal to a number of d6 rolls determined by the Potion's Rank, plus a modifier equal to its Rank (3d6+3). Once it regains Mana with one of these potions, it must regain Mana from a Rest to do so again.
